---
title: "Carolinas Regional Explorer — Application Technical Documentation"
subtitle: "Architecture, components, and maintenance guide"
date: last-modified
author: "Kailas Venkitasubramanian"
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
toc-location: left
number-sections: true
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    code-copy: true
    embed-resources: true
    mermaid:
      theme: neutral
---

::: callout-note
**Living document.** This describes the CRXP web application (`crxp/`). Update it whenever the
architecture, components, routes, build, or data contract change. Companion documents:
*Data Pipeline Technical Documentation* and *Methodology Guide* (both in `crxp-data/docs/`).
:::

# Overview

The **Carolinas Regional Explorer (CRXP)** is a community-data web application for the 14-county
Charlotte region (11 NC + 3 SC counties, ~752 Census tracts, 2020 vintage). It presents tract- and
county-level indicators as interactive choropleth maps with uncertainty-aware trend charts, bivariate
analysis, and spatial-cluster (LISA) analysis, plus researched per-indicator briefs, county profiles,
shareable printable reports, and open-data downloads (CSV/JSON per indicator + a complete zip).

The app is a **static site**: all data is precomputed by the data pipeline (`crxp-data/`) into a
documented JSON **data contract** under `static/data/`, and the app is prerendered to static HTML +
assets. There is no runtime backend or database.

## Tech stack

| Concern | Choice |
|---|---|
| Framework | **SvelteKit** with **Svelte 5 runes** (`$state`, `$derived`, `$effect`, `$props`) |
| Mapping | **MapLibre GL JS** (vector tiles + GeoJSON sources, feature-state value join) |
| Build / dev | **Vite**; **`@sveltejs/adapter-static`** (full prerender) |
| Search | **Fuse.js** (fuzzy area search) |
| Charts | Hand-rolled inline **SVG** (no chart library) |
| Hosting | **Netlify** (static) |
| Tests | **Vitest** (unit tests co-located as `*.test.js`) |

# High-level architecture

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
  subgraph Pipeline["crxp-data pipeline (separate repo)"]
    PL["Ingest, calc, spatial, warehouse"] --> EXP["Export / handoff"]
  end
  EXP -->|writes| DC[("static/data — data contract<br/>manifest, values, analytics,<br/>aggregates, areas, meta, geo")]

  subgraph App["crxp SvelteKit app"]
    DC -->|fetch at runtime| DL["lib/data/* loaders"]
    DL --> ST["lib/state/* rune stores"]
    ST -->|effect| MC["lib/map/MapController.js"]
    MC --> ML[("MapLibre GL map")]
    ST --> UI["lib/components/* + routes/*"]
    UI --> ML
  end

  BD["scripts/build-data.mjs"] -.validates.-> DC
  App -->|adapter-static prerender| OUT["build/ static site"]
  OUT --> NF["Netlify"]
```

**Key principle — separation of geometry and values.** GeoJSON layers carry only `geoid`; indicator
values are joined at render time via MapLibre **feature-state**. This keeps geometry files small and
lets the app swap indicators/years without reloading geometry.

**Key principle — no god service.** All imperative MapLibre calls live in one controller
(`MapController.js`). UI state lives in small **rune store slices**. The `/explore` page composes them:
state changes flow through `$effect` into the controller, which mutates the map.

# Repository structure

```
crxp/
├─ src/
│  ├─ lib/
│  │  ├─ components/     # Svelte UI components (map, legends, panels, charts)
│  │  ├─ data/          # data-contract loaders + pure transforms
│  │  ├─ state/         # Svelte 5 rune stores (*.svelte.js)
│  │  ├─ map/           # MapController + color/bivariate/lisa helpers
│  │  ├─ analytics/     # spatial.js (z, breaks, Pearson/Spearman) — pure stats helpers
│  │  ├─ content/       # editorial content (indicatorBriefs.js — researched "why it matters")
│  │  ├─ geo/           # point-in-polygon + geo helpers
│  │  ├─ search/        # Fuse-based area index
│  │  └─ util/          # url state, markdown, formatting, pins
│  ├─ routes/           # SvelteKit pages (file-based routing)
│  └─ app.css           # global tokens + styles
├─ static/
│  └─ data/             # THE DATA CONTRACT (produced by crxp-data)
├─ scripts/             # build-data.mjs (validator), build-data-zip.mjs (downloads), og/sitemap
├─ docs/                # this documentation (rendered to GitHub Pages via .github/workflows)
├─ .github/workflows/   # deploy-docs.yml (Quarto → GitHub Pages)
├─ vite.config.js (SvelteKit configured here) · netlify.toml
```

> SvelteKit is configured inside `vite.config.js` (the `sveltekit()` plugin sets the static adapter with
> `fallback: '404.html'` and runes mode); there is no separate `svelte.config.js`.

# The data contract (`static/data/`)

The app consumes a fixed set of JSON files. The pipeline is the **producer**; the app is the
**consumer**. See the *Data Pipeline Technical Documentation* for how these are generated.

| File | Purpose | Loaded by |
|---|---|---|
| `manifest.json` | Single source of truth: `schemaVersion`, `categories[]` (themes, with `color`/`order`) + `indicators[]` (id, slug, label, description, category, format, decimals, higherIsBetter, years, geoLevels, source, vintage, metaPath, hasZ, hasLisa) | `state/manifest.svelte.js` |
| `geo/tracts.geojson`, `geo/counties.geojson` | Geometry keyed by `geoid` only (counties are real dissolved polygons) | `MapController`, county profiles |
| `values/<id>.json` | `schemaVersion`, per-indicator `years`, `values{geoid:[…]}`, **`moe`/`cv`/`reliability`{geoid:[…]}**, shared `breaks`, `domain`, per-year `stats` | `data/values.js` |
| `analytics/z/<id>.json` | Per-year z-scores | `data/analytics.js` |
| `analytics/lisa/<id>.json` | Per-year LISA quadrants (FDR-corrected; computed by the pipeline) | `data/analytics.js` |
| `aggregates.json` | County + region **averages** per indicator/year, **plus `regionMoe`/`countyMoe`** (MOE of the mean) and breaks/domain | `data/aggregates.js` |
| `areas/{tracts,counties,places}.json` | Names/labels (+ county `bbox`) for search + profiles | `data/areas.js`, `search/areaIndex.js` |
| `meta/m<id>.md` | Per-indicator data-provenance markdown ("About the data") | `data/meta.js` |
| `provenance.json` | Library + GDAL/PROJ versions that produced the data (build provenance) | — (reference) |

The contract is validated by **`scripts/build-data.mjs`** (`npm run build:data`, also a Vitest fixture
test). It checks: a supported `schemaVersion`; each value file's internal consistency (years deep-equal
the manifest entry, value arrays match `years` length and contain only `number|null`, per-year stats with
breaks/min/max/p1/p99); analytics presence + content (z/LISA years aligned, LISA quadrants in 0–4);
aggregates present + year-aligned; and geoid reconciliation against the area manifest. Malformed JSON
fails cleanly. The full app build runs this first.

::: callout-tip
**Uncertainty in the contract.** `values/<id>.json` carries per-tract `moe`/`cv`/`reliability`, and
`aggregates.json` carries `regionMoe`/`countyMoe`. The app surfaces these as error bands on the **tract,
county, and region** trend lines plus reliability badges, and an optional on-map hatch for unreliable
tracts. Indicators without sampling MOE (raster/VIIRS, and the legacy *private school* indicator) omit
them and the UI degrades gracefully.
:::

::: callout-note
**Editorial content (not in the contract).** Plain-language, cited "why this matters" briefs for every
indicator live in `src/lib/content/indicatorBriefs.js` (keyed by slug: `why` markdown + verified
`resources`). They are maintained in the app repo, independent of the pipeline, and power the indicator
pages and the explore "About this indicator" panel.
:::

# State management (rune stores)

Each store is a `*.svelte.js` module exporting a `$state` object plus mutator functions.

| Store | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `manifest.svelte.js` | Loads `manifest.json` once; exposes categories + indicators; lookup by id/slug |
| `explorer.svelte.js` | Current indicator, year, geo level (`tract`/`county`); setters |
| `selection.svelte.js` | Hovered geoid, multi-select `selectedIds[]`, legend class filter |
| `analysis.svelte.js` | Analysis mode (`explore` / `bivariate` / `cluster`) + mode params |
| `pins.svelte.js` | Pinned areas for comparison |

URL state (indicator, year, geo, mode, selection, pins) is serialized/restored via `util/url.js` so
views are deep-linkable.

# Map layer

`lib/map/MapController.js` is the **only** module that talks to MapLibre imperatively. It:

- builds the map, tract/county fill + line layers, and basemap/overlay handling;
- joins values via **feature-state** (`setFeatureState` per geoid) rather than re-styling per feature;
- applies a **step color expression** built from the indicator's shared breaks and the theme color
  ramp (`map/colorScale.js → themeRamp`, `buildStepExpression`, `legendClasses`);
- supports legend-class **filtering/dimming**, hover, multi-select, and boundary highlight;
- renders an **uncertainty hatch** layer (`<level>-hatch`, a canvas-generated diagonal pattern with
  `fill-pattern`) over tracts whose `reliability` feature-state is `unreliable` (CV > 30%), shown only
  in explore mode and toggleable via `setReliabilityVisible` (Map settings → "Flag unreliable areas",
  **off by default**);
- swaps into bivariate (3×3, `map/bivariate.js`) and LISA (`map/lisaColors.js`) styling for the
  analysis modes.

`colorScale.js` also provides `formatValue(value, format, decimals)` used across charts/legends
(`number`, `percent`, `dollar`, `years`, etc.).

# Components

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  EX["explore page"] --> MV[MapView]
  EX --> AMB[AnalysisModeBar]
  EX --> IB[IndicatorBrowser]
  EX --> YS[YearSlider]
  EX --> GLT[GeoLevelToggle]
  EX --> LG["Legend / LisaLegend / BivariateLegend"]
  EX --> TC[TrendChart]
  EX --> AS[AreaSearch]
  EX --> CP[ComparePanel]
  EX --> MS[MapSettings]
  EX --> BP["BivariatePanel / LisaPanel"]
```

| Component | Role |
|---|---|
| `MapView` | Hosts the map element; delegates to `MapController` |
| `AnalysisModeBar` | Always-visible switch between Explore / Bivariate / Spatial clusters |
| `IndicatorBrowser` | Theme accordion (collapsed by default) + search; indicators sorted **alphabetically within each theme**; a "Quality of Life Dimensions" sub-heading separates Character (general demographics) from the rest |
| `IndicatorSelect` | Reusable `<select>` that groups indicators into **theme-colored `<optgroup>`s** (alphabetical within); used by the bivariate and LISA panels |
| `YearSlider` | Scrubs the active year (per-indicator `years`) |
| `GeoLevelToggle` | Tract vs county view |
| `Legend` / `LisaLegend` / `BivariateLegend` | Class legends; hoverable filtering (incl. bivariate cell → tract filter) |
| `TrendChart` | Tract vs county vs region SVG trend; **MOE error bands on all three lines** (tract MOE; county/region from `aggregates` `countyMoe`/`regionMoe`), ±MOE readout, reliability badge; aggregate lines labeled "(avg of tracts)" |
| Trend float (in `/explore`) | For a selected tract: **comparable-period change markers** — Δ for the **5-yr and 10-yr** non-overlapping periods each with a 90% significance flag (`data/series.js → comparableChanges`, periods derived from the latest year) — plus **provenance badges** (vintage, *model-based* for CDC PLACES, *pre-2020 harmonized* for ACS). The x-axis is **year-proportional** so uneven cadence renders truthfully |
| `BivariateScatter` | z-score scatter with marginal distributions, regression line, **Pearson + Spearman** (descriptive), two-way map↔dot hover + neighborhood name |
| `AreaSearch` | Fuse-based search across tracts/counties/places |
| `ComparePanel` / `PinButton` | Pin/compare areas |
| `MapSettings` | Basemap choice, transparency, reliability-hatch toggle |
| `CountyLocator` | Inset/locator map drawing the 14 real county polygons, active one highlighted, each a link (doubles as a switcher) |
| `CountySwitcher` | Prev/next + jump-to dropdown across the 14 county profiles |
| `MetricInfoPanel` / `MarkdownBlock` | Render an indicator's brief; compact (sidebar) shows the lead paragraph + a "Full details & sources" link, full (indicator page) adds cited resources + "About the data" |
| `ProfileHero`, `MetricCardGrid`, `PercentileStrip`, `Sparkline`, `DumbbellChart` | County/city profile + report visuals |
| `AttributionFooter` | Standard "Carolinas Regional Explorer · UNC Charlotte Urban Institute · (year)" footer on shareable/printable artifacts (profiles, reports) |
| `Seo`, `ShareButtons` | Metadata + social sharing (profiles **and** custom reports) |

# Analysis modes

- **Explore** — single-indicator choropleth with shared cross-year breaks (so color = value is stable
  across years), legend filtering, hover/selection, trend chart.
- **Bivariate** — two indicators on a 3×3 tercile grid (rank terciles of raw values); hovering a grid
  cell filters matching tracts. The side panel shows a **correlation scatter** (`BivariateScatter`):
  standardized (z-score) values of the two indicators, dots colored by their 3×3 class, marginal
  distributions, a regression line, and **both Pearson and Spearman** correlations labeled *descriptive
  only* (spatial autocorrelation precludes a naïve significance reading). Hovering a dot highlights the
  tract on the map (`MapController.setHoverId`) and vice-versa, and shows that tract's **neighborhood
  name** above the scatter.
- **Spatial clusters (LISA)** — local Moran's I quadrants (High-High, Low-Low, High-Low, Low-High,
  not significant) from `analytics/lisa/<id>.json`, **computed once in the pipeline** with a
  without-replacement conditional-permutation null (999 permutations) and **Benjamini–Hochberg FDR**.

LISA and z-scores are **precomputed by the pipeline** and consumed as-is (the app has no client-side LISA
implementation — that avoids a second statistical code path drifting from the source of truth).
`lib/analytics/spatial.js` keeps only the pure helpers the app still needs at runtime: z-scores, quantile
breaks (for bivariate terciles), and Pearson/Spearman for the scatter.

# Routing & pages

File-based routes under `src/routes/`:

| Route | Description | Prerender |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | Landing page | yes |
| `/explore` | Main map explorer (deep-linkable via query state); sidebar "About this indicator" shows the brief lead + a link to the full page | yes |
| `/indicators`, `/indicators/[slug]` | Indicator catalog (alphabetical within theme, Character/QoL split) + per-indicator pages — **"Why this matters" brief, cited "Learn more" resources, "About the data"** | yes (one per indicator) |
| `/county/[fips]` | County profiles — real-polygon **inset/locator map**, **14-county switcher**, metric cards, share buttons + attribution footer; OG cards | yes (one per county) |
| `/report` | Custom multi-tract printable report — **social share buttons** + attribution footer; selection encoded in the URL (`?tracts=…&geo=…`) | yes |
| `/data` | **Downloads page** — collapsible dimensions revealing a per-indicator data dictionary with **CSV + JSON** downloads, plus **"Download all (.zip)"** (a prebuilt static archive) | yes |
| `/methods`, `/about` | Reference pages (Methods explains MOE/reliability, rolling 5-yr, FDR-LISA, unweighted aggregates) | yes |

All pages are prerendered by adapter-static; `/indicators/[slug]` and `/county/[fips]` enumerate their
entries from the manifest/areas at build time.

# Build, dev & deploy

```bash
npm install
npm run dev          # local dev server (Vite)
npm run build:data   # validate the data contract only (fast gate)
npm run build:zip    # prebuild the download artifacts (per-indicator CSVs + data-dictionary + crxp-data.zip)
npm run build        # build:data + build:zip + Vite build (prerender) → build/
npm run build:full   # build:data + build:zip + OG cards + sitemap + Vite build (production)
npm run preview      # serve the production build locally
npm test             # vitest
```

- **Downloads are prebuilt:** `build:zip` (`scripts/build-data-zip.mjs`) writes `static/downloads/`
  (`csv/<slug>.csv`, `data-dictionary.csv`, `README.txt`, `crxp-data.zip`) from the data contract on
  every build, so the `/data` page serves them as static files (git-ignored, regenerated each build).
  Uses `jszip` (a dev/build dependency only).
- **Deploy (app):** Netlify runs `npm run build:full` and serves `build/`. `netlify.toml` sets
  `Cache-Control` on `/data/*` (~1 hour), so updated data can take up to that long to appear after a deploy.
- **Deploy (docs):** this documentation is rendered from Quarto and published to **GitHub Pages** by
  `.github/workflows/deploy-docs.yml` on push (repo Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions).
- **Adapter:** `adapter-static` with full prerender (`fallback: '404.html'`); the app must remain
  prerenderable (no server-only endpoints).

# Updating the DATA shown in the app

Data is produced by the pipeline and handed off; the app itself rarely changes when indicators change.

1. In `crxp-data`, run the pipeline for the indicator(s) **with `--handoff`** — this writes/merges the
   value, analytics, meta, **manifest**, **aggregates**, and **provenance.json** files into `crxp/static/data/`.
2. Run `npm run build:data` (or `node scripts/build-data.mjs`) to validate the contract.
3. Run `npm run build` (which also regenerates the download artifacts), review with `npm run preview`, deploy.
4. For a brand-new indicator, also add a plain-language brief to `src/lib/content/indicatorBriefs.js`
   (the indicator page falls back to the `meta` markdown if no brief exists yet).

Adding a brand-new indicator requires **no app code change** — the manifest entry written by the
pipeline makes it appear in the relevant theme automatically (themes are fixed in the manifest
`categories`). See the *Data Pipeline Technical Documentation* → "Add a new indicator".

# Conventions for maintainers

- **Svelte 5 runes** throughout; prefer `$derived` over manual effects; keep effects in the page that
  composes stores, not deep in components.
- **One controller** for the map (`MapController.js`); components never call MapLibre directly.
- **Geometry is value-free**; never bake values into GeoJSON — join via feature-state.
- **Pure transforms** in `lib/data/*` (e.g. `series.js`) are unit-tested; keep them side-effect free.
- Match existing file size/responsibility boundaries; co-locate `*.test.js`.
- When you add a feature that consumes a new contract field, update `build-data.mjs` validation and
  this document.

# Testing

- **Unit:** Vitest over pure modules (`series.js`, `spatial.js` incl. z/quantile/**Pearson/Spearman**,
  `colorScale.js`, `bivariate.js`, `contract.js`, neighborhoods, search, url, pins, geo).
- **Contract:** `scripts/build-data.mjs` (`crossCheck`) is the integration gate for the data contract,
  exercised both against the live `static/data` and a synthetic fixture in `build-data.test.js`.
- **Visual/e2e:** manual + Playwright spot-checks during data updates (verify a refreshed indicator
  renders and shows the expected year/MOE).
